title: She’s running Boston with her best friend after both diagnosed with cancer
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Meg Gannon, 47, from Canton, Mass., was diagnosed with breast cancer on February 8, 2024.
Gannon will run the Boston Marathon for Mass General on April 20, 2026, after recovering from a double mastectomy.
Gannon's best friend, Peggy, also a cancer survivor, will join her in the marathon, honoring all cancer patients.

title: ‘SNL’ recap: Best moments from Jack Black hosting ‘Saturday Night Live’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Jack Black hosted 'SNL' for the fifth time on April 4, 2026, joining the five-timers club.
Jack White acted as musical guest for the episode, marking his fifth appearance in this role.
Black performed a parody of 'Seven Nation Army' alongside White, energizing the show's opening segment.

title: Jaylen Brown opens up about past criticisms of his left hand: ‘There was some truth to it’
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Jaylen Brown addressed past criticisms about his left hand, acknowledging struggles due to a serious wrist injury.
Brown tore all the ligaments in his left hand in 2021 and reinjured it in 2023, impacting his gameplay.
Since recovering, Brown significantly improved, averaging 28.7 points, 7.0 rebounds, and 5.3 assists this season.

title: ‘The Red Sox were my dream job’: Don Orsillo reflects on NESN tenure, reunites with former colleagues
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Don Orsillo reflects on his NESN tenure as he returns to Boston with the San Diego Padres.
Orsillo was the Red Sox play-by-play announcer from 2001 to 2015, now calls games for the Padres.
Former NESN colleagues Jim Rice and Tom Caron reunite with Orsillo during his Boston visit.

title: Alex Cora explains his decision to slot Roman Anthony at DH early this season
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox manager Alex Cora opted for Roman Anthony as DH due to defensive shortcomings early in the season.
Anthony showed offensive strength with a triple but struggled defensively in a recent game against the Padres.
Cora defended Anthony's improved effort on a failed play, acknowledging his need for further defensive improvement.

title: Connelly Early gave Red Sox a chance to win despite high pitch count, lack of command
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox pitcher Connelly Early limited the Padres to two runs despite walking four batters in four innings.
San Diego's winning run in the ninth inning led to a 3-2 loss for the Red Sox at home.
Early tied a Red Sox record with 39 strikeouts in his first six career regular season games.

title: 4 takeaways from the Revolution’s 3-0 win over CF Montreal
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: New England Revolution defeated CF Montreal 3-0, with goals from Luca Langoni, Mamadou Fofana, and Peyton Miller.
Revolution head coach Marko Mitrović received a red card for leaving the technical area during the 34th minute.
Peyton Miller, an academy graduate, scored his second goal of the season to secure the win for the Revolution.

title: Here’s the latest on Red Sox’ Triston Casas, Johan Oviedo injuries
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Triston Casas will resume hitting from a tee Monday, recovering from a rib injury incurred in late March.
Johan Oviedo will meet his Tommy John surgeon for an elbow issue after velocity dropped in his last game.
Kutter Crawford and Patrick Sandoval near rehab assignments after missing the 2025 season due to injuries.

title: This 22-year-old cancer survivor is running Boston for MGH
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Grace Taylor, a 22-year-old cancer survivor from Dedham, is running the Boston Marathon for Mass General Hospital.
Taylor was diagnosed with aggressive thyroid cancer at 19, and credits her survival to doctors at Mass General.
Taylor aims to honor her doctors and the AYA program, drawing on her experience as a young adult cancer survivor.

title: South Boston runner supports BMC social workers in Boston Marathon
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Paige Beattie, 23, from South Boston, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon with Team BMC.
Beattie aims to raise $10,000 to support Boston Medical Center's social workers and their safety-net mission.
Fundraising activities include HandleBar classes and Stats Bar events to boost BMC resources.
